evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: building both traditional data pipelines and intelligent, agentic solutions that create real business impact; apply data modelling methodologies
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Classified as mixed because 'agentic solutions that create real business impact' signals velocity/innovation alongside 'data modelling methodologies' and 'advanced data reporting' as rigour signals, with genuine equal weight.
evidence.domain_risk: analytics consultancy serving over 50 clients across various sectors
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Classified as moderate because Gemma Analytics is a consultancy where no sector-specific regulatory or financial reporting language appears in the JD — errors affect client business decisions without named compliance consequence.
evidence.data_team_maturity: team of 18, growing to 24 in 2026; over 50 clients served
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Classified as mid because the firm has an established client base and is actively growing (18→24), with named tools implied in the modern data stack, but too small for mature classification.
evidence.jd_authorship: work with multiple technologies across the modern data stack; develop advanced data reporting and visualisations
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Classified as recruiter because responsibilities are generic bullets without named tools, specific methods, or context that would distinguish this role from any other AE/analytics role.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: intelligent, agentic solutions that create real business impact; stakeholder/client management experience
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Classified as commercial because the role's primary output targets external clients, with 'client management' listed as optional and 'business impact' as the framing measure.
evidence.autonomy_level: building both traditional data pipelines and intelligent, agentic solutions
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Classified as mixed because the role involves hands-on execution (building pipelines) alongside strategic latitude implied by the small team context and agentic solution design.
evidence.ai_role: leverage AI tools including agentic workflows and AI coding assistants; professional AI tool experience, ideally including coding assistants
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Classified as ai_user because the candidate is expected to 'leverage AI tools including agentic workflows and AI coding assistants' — the AI accelerates the candidate's own engineering work.
evidence.testing_framing: No testing or data quality signal anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Classified as absent because no testing tools, data contracts, observability, or data quality practices are mentioned anywhere in the posting.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Classified as none because the JD is entirely framed in delivery and capability terms with no risk, compliance, or stakeholder-trust language.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: building both traditional data pipelines and intelligent, agentic solutions
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Classified as mixed because the role spans both building new agentic solutions (greenfield) and developing standard data pipelines and reporting (fix_scale) across client engagements.
evidence.language_gate: Native or C2 fluency in German and English
